question: when was the last time anyone was on the moon
answer: The last time anyone was on the moon was during the Apollo 17 mission in December 1972. Astronauts Eugene Cernan and Harrison Schmitt spent about three days on the lunar surface, while Ron Evans orbited above in the Command Module. Since then, no human has returned to the moon. The Apollo program, which was responsible for these lunar missions, was officially terminated in 1975. question: who wrote he ain't heavy he's my brother lyrics
answer: The song "He Ain't Heavy, He's My Brother" was written by Bobby Scott and Bob Russell. The Hollies recorded a popular version of the song in 1969. The song's title and lyrics express the idea that helping and supporting others is a shared responsibility and not a burden. question: when did the isle of wight become an island
answer: The Isle of Wight has been an island for millions of years. It is located in the English Channel, off the south coast of England. question: what is the maximum data rate for the 802.11a standard select one
answer: The maximum data rate for the 802.11a standard is 54 Mbps. This standard operates in the 5 GHz frequency band and uses orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) encoding. It was introduced in 1999 as part of the 802.11 set of wireless networking standards. Other common 802.11 standards include 802.11b (11 Mbps), 802.11g (54 Mbps), and 802.11n
